metaDescription: Accountants for contractors and freelancers. Fixed-fee IR35 support, limited company vs umbrella advice and tax-efficient pay. ACCA-qualified, switch in days.
title: Accountants for Contractors & Freelancers
intro: Contracting should reward the risk you take, not bury you in admin and IR35 worry. We give contractors, freelancers and personal service companies a dedicated named accountant, a fixed monthly fee and cloud-first books — so your take-home is sound, your status is documented and HMRC never has a reason to come knocking.
highlights: Clear IR35 status reviews and contract pointers
Limited company or umbrella — honest, numbers-led advice
Tax-efficient salary and dividend planning
Allowable expenses checked and kept compliant
Company pension contributions factored in
Cloud bookkeeping in Xero, QuickBooks or Sage
Fixed monthly fee with your own accountant

sections.heading: IR35 and off-payroll working, in plain English
body: IR35 decides whether HMRC treats you as genuinely self-employed or as a disguised employee for a particular engagement. Get it wrong and the tax difference is significant, so it pays to understand who decides and why.

In the private sector, since April 2021 a medium or large end client is responsible for deciding your status and must issue a Status Determination Statement. Where the end client qualifies as small, the decision stays with your own limited company. In the public sector, the client always decides.
bullets: Inside IR35: the engagement is taxed much like employment, with PAYE and National Insurance applied
Outside IR35: you can usually work through your own limited company in the normal way
Status turns on control, personal service and substitution, and mutuality of obligation
HMRC's CEST tool is a useful starting point, not the final word

sections.heading: Limited company or umbrella?
body: If most of your work falls outside IR35, your own limited company is often the more tax-efficient route and gives you full control. If you are routinely inside IR35 or contracting short term, an umbrella can be simpler, with less paperwork to manage.
There is no one-size-fits-all answer. We look at your contracts, your rate and your plans, then recommend the structure that genuinely works for you rather than the one that suits us.

sections.heading: Paying yourself tax-efficiently
body: Through a limited company, profit is usually taken as a mix of salary and dividends, planned around your wider income so you keep more of what you earn. We map this out with you each year and keep it under review as the rules and your circumstances change.
bullets: A considered salary and dividend split for your situation
Only expenses incurred wholly and exclusively for the business claimed
Pension contributions made by the company where it makes sense
Some contractors benefit from the VAT Flat Rate Scheme — we will tell you if it fits

sections.heading: Cloud-first, so you always know your position
body: We set up and run your books in Xero, QuickBooks or Sage, with bank feeds reconciled regularly so your numbers are always current. You can raise invoices, snap receipts and see your take-home from your phone, while we handle the filings in the background.
It also means your IR35 position, income and dividends are documented properly — exactly what you want on file if a status is ever questioned.

sections.heading: Switch to MMR in days, not weeks
body: Moving to us is paperless and usually takes just a few days. With your permission we contact your previous accountant, obtain your records under standard professional clearance and get everything across, so there is no gap in your compliance and nothing for you to chase.

sections.heading: Chartered-standard advice you can trust
body: We are ACCA-qualified, work to chartered standards and carry full professional indemnity insurance. With our office in Manchester and everything in the cloud, you get the same proactive, named-accountant service wherever you contract across the UK.

faqs.question: Who decides my IR35 status?
faqs.answer: It depends on the end client. In the private sector, a medium or large client decides and issues a Status Determination Statement; if the client is small, your own limited company decides. In the public sector, the client always makes the determination.

faqs.question: What does it mean to be inside or outside IR35?
faqs.answer: Inside IR35 means the engagement is treated much like employment, with PAYE and National Insurance deducted. Outside IR35 means you can usually work through your own limited company in the normal way. Each contract is assessed on its own facts.

faqs.question: Should I use a limited company or an umbrella?
faqs.answer: If your work is generally outside IR35, a limited company is often more tax-efficient. If you are routinely inside IR35 or contracting briefly, an umbrella can be simpler. We will look at your contracts and rate and give you a clear, honest recommendation.

faqs.question: What expenses can I claim as a contractor?
faqs.answer: Broadly, costs incurred wholly and exclusively for the business — things like business travel, professional subscriptions and certain equipment. We review your spending so you claim everything you are entitled to without straying outside the rules.

faqs.question: Can my company pay into my pension?
faqs.answer: Yes. Pension contributions can be made by your company and are a popular, tax-efficient way to extract profit. We will factor this into your overall pay plan. This is general guidance, not personal advice — we will tailor it to you.

metaDescription: Ecommerce accountants for Shopify, Amazon, eBay and Etsy sellers. VAT, marketplace rules, payouts and stock handled. Cloud-first, fixed fees, switch in days.
title: Ecommerce Accountants for Online Sellers
intro: Selling online is a numbers game across more channels than ever. We help Shopify, Amazon, eBay and Etsy sellers make sense of payouts, fees, refunds and VAT — with a dedicated named accountant, a fixed monthly fee and cloud books that tie every platform together so you always know your true margin.
highlights: Multi-channel payouts, fees and refunds reconciled
VAT registration and the right scheme for your shop
Marketplace deemed-supplier rules explained
OSS and IOSS guidance for EU sales
Postponed VAT accounting for imports
Stock and cost of goods sold done properly
Integrations such as A2X and live cloud reporting

sections.heading: Selling across channels, reconciled to the penny
body: Every marketplace pays you differently, and the headline payout is rarely the real picture once selling fees, advertising, refunds and reserves are stripped out. Without a tidy system it is easy to mistake turnover for profit.

We connect your stores and reconcile each settlement, often using tools like A2X, so sales, fees and refunds land in the right place. The result is books you can trust and a clear view of what each channel actually earns you.

sections.heading: VAT for online sellers, made manageable
body: VAT is where most e-commerce businesses come unstuck. You must register once your taxable turnover passes the £90,000 threshold, and from there choosing and running the right scheme matters for both cash flow and compliance.
We handle registration, returns and Making Tax Digital filing, and we check whether a scheme such as the Flat Rate Scheme suits your margins before you commit.

sections.heading: Marketplaces, imports and the deemed-supplier rules
body: For certain sales, online marketplaces are treated as the deemed supplier and account for the VAT themselves — for example goods imported in consignments of £135 or less, and some sales made by overseas sellers. Knowing when this applies keeps your own returns accurate.
bullets: Goods imported in consignments of £135 or less can fall under the marketplace as deemed supplier
Some sales by overseas sellers are also handled by the marketplace
Postponed VAT accounting lets you account for import VAT on your return rather than paying it upfront
We reconcile marketplace-collected VAT against your own records

sections.heading: Selling into the EU after Brexit
body: Cross-border sales bring customs, duties and extra VAT steps. For B2C sales to EU consumers, the One Stop Shop can simplify how you report VAT, and for imports into the EU of €150 or less the Import One Stop Shop can do the same.
We help you work out which scheme fits, keep your customs and duty position in order, and make sure post-Brexit paperwork does not slow your shipments.

sections.heading: Stock, margins and the numbers that matter
body: Inventory is usually your biggest cost, so getting stock accounting and cost of goods sold right is what turns a busy shop into a profitable one. We track these properly and translate them into live cloud reporting on margin, cash and your best-selling lines.
With clean numbers you can price with confidence, plan reorders and see which products are quietly losing you money.

sections.heading: Cloud-first and easy to switch
body: We run your books in Xero, QuickBooks or Sage with your sales channels feeding in automatically, so reporting is always current. Moving to us is paperless and usually takes a few days — we collect your records under professional clearance and set everything up for you.
We are ACCA-qualified, work to chartered standards and are fully insured, with our office in Manchester supporting online sellers right across the UK.

faqs.question: When do I need to register for VAT?
faqs.answer: You must register once your taxable turnover passes the £90,000 threshold, though you can register voluntarily before that if it helps you reclaim input VAT. We monitor your turnover and handle registration and returns when the time comes.

faqs.question: Do marketplaces collect VAT for me?
faqs.answer: Sometimes. For certain sales — such as goods imported in consignments of £135 or less, and some sales by overseas sellers — the marketplace is treated as the deemed supplier and accounts for the VAT. We make sure this is reflected correctly in your own records.

faqs.question: What are OSS and IOSS?
faqs.answer: They are EU VAT simplifications. The One Stop Shop helps with B2C sales to EU consumers, while the Import One Stop Shop can simplify VAT on imports into the EU of €150 or less. We advise on whether registering for either makes sense for your sales.

faqs.question: How do you handle Amazon and Shopify payouts?
faqs.answer: We connect your channels and reconcile each settlement, often using an integration such as A2X, so sales, fees, refunds and reserves are recorded accurately. That gives you a true view of profit per channel rather than just the cash that lands in your bank.

faqs.question: Can you help with importing stock from abroad?
faqs.answer: Yes. We guide you through postponed VAT accounting for import VAT, explain the customs and duty position on cross-border goods, and keep the paperwork in order so your stock keeps moving. We will tailor this to how and where you source.

metaTitle: Startup Accountants & R&D Tax Relief | MMR
metaDescription: Startup accountants for UK tech founders. R&D tax relief, SEIS/EIS, EMI options and investor-ready accounts. Fixed fees, a dedicated accountant, cloud-first.
title: Startup Accountants for Founders & Tech
intro: Early-stage companies need more than compliance — they need numbers investors believe and reliefs that stretch the runway. We give founders a dedicated named accountant, a fixed monthly fee and cloud-first finance, covering R&D tax relief, SEIS and EIS, EMI schemes and the investor-ready reporting that makes funding rounds run smoothly.
highlights: R&D tax relief claims under the current rules
SEIS and EIS advance assurance support
EMI share option schemes to retain key staff
Investor-ready management accounts and forecasts
Cap-table support for rounds and due diligence
Patent Box advice for patented inventions
Fixed monthly fee with a dedicated accountant

sections.heading: R&D tax relief, claimed properly
body: If your company is solving genuine technical problems, R&D tax relief can return real cash to reinvest. The regime has changed, so claims need to be built carefully and backed by evidence that stands up to HMRC scrutiny.

For accounting periods beginning on or after 1 April 2024, most companies claim through the new merged R&D expenditure credit scheme. Loss-making, R&D-intensive SMEs may qualify for extra support under the enhanced intensive scheme, ERIS. We identify qualifying work, prepare the figures and document the technical narrative.

sections.heading: Raising investment with SEIS and EIS
body: SEIS and EIS make your company far more attractive to investors by offering them generous income tax relief, and securing HMRC advance assurance before you raise gives everyone confidence the relief should apply.
We help you check eligibility, apply for advance assurance and complete the compliance paperwork so investors receive the certificates they need.
bullets: SEIS: up to £250,000 can be raised, with 50% income tax relief for investors
EIS: investors can claim 30% income tax relief
Advance assurance from HMRC reassures investors before they commit
We handle the compliance statements that trigger investors' certificates

sections.heading: Rewarding your team with EMI
body: Attracting talent on a startup budget is hard, so share options often bridge the gap. Enterprise Management Incentive schemes are a tax-advantaged way to reward and retain the key people who help you grow.
We set up your EMI scheme, handle the valuation and reporting, and keep it compliant as your team and cap table evolve.

sections.heading: Investor-ready from day one
body: When you raise or face due diligence, scrappy spreadsheets cost you credibility and time. We produce management accounts, forecasts and cap-table support that stand up to investor questions and let you walk into the room prepared.
Between rounds, the same numbers help you watch burn, runway and the metrics that matter, so you make decisions on evidence rather than instinct.

sections.heading: Protecting the value you create
body: If your innovation is patented, Patent Box can reduce corporation tax to 10% on profits earned from the patented invention. It is easy to overlook, so we flag it early and help you claim it where it applies, keeping more profit inside the business to reinvest.

sections.heading: Cloud-first, chartered-standard, easy to join
body: We run your books in Xero, QuickBooks or Sage, so your live numbers are always ready for a board pack or an investor update. Switching to us is paperless and usually takes a few days, with records collected under professional clearance.
We are ACCA-qualified, work to chartered standards and are fully insured, with our office in Manchester supporting founders across the UK.

faqs.question: How does R&D tax relief work now?
faqs.answer: For accounting periods beginning on or after 1 April 2024, most companies claim through the new merged R&D expenditure credit scheme. Loss-making, R&D-intensive SMEs may access extra support under ERIS. We assess your work, prepare the claim and document the technical case.

faqs.question: What is the difference between SEIS and EIS?
faqs.answer: Both encourage investment by giving investors income tax relief. Under SEIS a company can raise up to £250,000 with 50% relief for investors; under EIS investors can claim 30% relief. We help you secure advance assurance and stay compliant with each scheme.

faqs.question: What is advance assurance and do I need it?
faqs.answer: Advance assurance is HMRC's indication that your share issue should qualify for SEIS or EIS. It is not compulsory, but most investors expect it because it reassures them the relief should be available. We prepare and submit the application for you.

faqs.question: Can you help me set up an EMI scheme?
faqs.answer: Yes. Enterprise Management Incentive schemes are a tax-advantaged way to reward and retain key staff with share options. We handle the set-up, valuation and ongoing reporting, and keep the scheme compliant as your company grows.

faqs.question: What is Patent Box?
faqs.answer: Patent Box is a relief that can reduce corporation tax to 10% on profits arising from patented inventions. If you hold qualifying patents it can be valuable, so we check whether it applies and help you claim it. This is general guidance, not personal advice.

metaDescription: Landlord and property accountants for UK rental income. Section 24, company structuring, CGT and MTD for landlords handled. Fixed fees, a dedicated accountant.
title: Landlord & Property Accountants
intro: Property tax has changed fast, and the right structure now makes a real difference to what you keep. We help landlords and property investors with rental accounts, Section 24, incorporation questions, Capital Gains Tax and the move to Making Tax Digital — all with a dedicated named accountant, a fixed monthly fee and cloud-first books.
highlights: Rental accounts and allowable expenses, done right
Section 24 finance-cost relief explained
Personal name vs limited company structuring
Capital Gains Tax on property reported on time
Making Tax Digital for Income Tax readiness
Replacement of domestic items relief claimed
Fixed monthly fee with your own accountant

sections.heading: Section 24 and the cost of your mortgage
body: For residential property held in your own name, you can no longer simply deduct mortgage interest from your rental profit. Under Section 24, finance costs instead give a basic-rate 20% tax reducer, which can push higher-rate landlords into a larger bill than the headline profit suggests.
We calculate this correctly, show you the real effect on your position and use it as the starting point for any conversation about how your portfolio is best held.

sections.heading: Personal name or limited company?
body: Holding property through a limited company allows the full interest deduction that Section 24 removes for individuals, which is why so many landlords ask about incorporating. It is not automatically the right move, though — a company brings corporation tax and decisions about how to extract profit, and moving existing property in has its own costs.
We model both routes for your circumstances so the choice is based on numbers, not headlines.
bullets: A company can deduct mortgage interest in full, unlike individually-held property
Profits are subject to corporation tax, with planning needed to extract them
Stamp Duty Land Tax can apply when you move property into a company
SDLT higher rates for additional dwellings are 5%, increased from 3% on 31 October 2024

sections.heading: Capital Gains Tax when you sell
body: Selling a residential property comes with a tight deadline that catches many landlords out, so it pays to plan the sale rather than react to it.
We prepare the figures, claim the reliefs you are entitled to and make sure you report and pay on time.
bullets: UK residential property disposals must be reported and paid within 60 days
CGT on residential property is charged at 18% (basic rate) and 24% (higher rate)
The annual exempt amount is £3,000
We plan disposals to use allowances and reliefs efficiently

sections.heading: Getting ready for Making Tax Digital
body: Digital record-keeping is coming to property income, so tidy, cloud-based books are quickly becoming essential rather than optional. Making Tax Digital for Income Tax starts in April 2026 for landlords and sole traders with income over £50,000, then extends to those with income over £30,000 from April 2027.
We get your records into the cloud now, so quarterly updates become routine rather than a scramble when the rules bite.

sections.heading: Claiming everything you are entitled to
body: Small reliefs add up across a portfolio, and missed ones quietly inflate your tax bill year after year. We make sure your allowable expenses are captured in full and that you claim replacement of domestic items relief when you replace furnishings and white goods.
We also keep you up to date as the regime evolves — for example, the Furnished Holiday Lettings rules were abolished from April 2025, which changes the position for many holiday-let owners.

sections.heading: Chartered-standard, cloud-first, easy to switch
body: We run your property books in Xero, QuickBooks or Sage so your numbers are always current, whether you own one flat or a growing portfolio. Joining us is paperless and usually takes a few days, with your records collected under professional clearance.
We are ACCA-qualified, work to chartered standards and are fully insured, with our office in Manchester supporting landlords across the UK.

faqs.question: What is Section 24 and how does it affect me?
faqs.answer: Section 24 restricts finance-cost relief on individually-held residential property to a basic-rate 20% tax reducer, rather than a full deduction from rental profit. For higher-rate landlords this can mean a larger tax bill than profit alone suggests. We calculate the real impact for you.

faqs.question: Should I put my property into a limited company?
faqs.answer: It depends. A company can deduct mortgage interest in full, but it brings corporation tax, profit-extraction decisions and possible Stamp Duty Land Tax on moving property in. We model both options for your situation so you can decide based on the numbers.

faqs.question: When do I have to report Capital Gains Tax on a property sale?
faqs.answer: A UK residential property disposal must be reported and the tax paid within 60 days. CGT on residential property is charged at 18% for basic-rate and 24% for higher-rate taxpayers, with a £3,000 annual exempt amount. We prepare and file this for you.

faqs.question: When does Making Tax Digital start for landlords?
faqs.answer: Making Tax Digital for Income Tax begins in April 2026 for landlords and sole traders with income over £50,000, and from April 2027 for those with income over £30,000. We get your records into the cloud early so quarterly reporting is straightforward.

faqs.question: What expenses can I claim against my rental income?
faqs.answer: Allowable running costs of letting — and replacement of domestic items relief when you replace furnishings and white goods. The Furnished Holiday Lettings regime was abolished from April 2025, which affects holiday lets. We make sure you claim correctly for your properties.
